concerns.text: Task T021 implements `baseline_runner.py` for the 1.5B distilled model. This silently relaxes FR-004 and Constitution Principle VII, which mandate comparing FastContext-Lite against the 'original FastContext (4B model on CPU)'. Substituting the 4B baseline with a 1.5B model without explicit spec authorization violates the comparative success criteria (SC-001, SC-002) and constitutes a scope violation.

FR-005 explicitly requires performing a 'paired t-test' on the 'Regular' dataset. While T027 selects the test type and T028 calculates results, there is no explicit task to execute the paired t-test specifically for the Regular set comparison. The current tasks conflate test selection with execution, risking the omission of the mandatory paired t-test step required by the spec.

FR-006 and SC-004 require calculating performance degradation of FastContext-Lite relative to the 'FastContext' (4B) baseline on the 'Irregular' set. The plan's risk mitigation (and implied task scope) suggests analyzing the Irregular set against a 'theoretical optimum' if the 4B baseline is too slow. Task T029 lacks the explicit instruction to compare against the 4B baseline, effectively making the success criterion unmeasurable under the proposed fallback.
